name: Open-Face Steamed Dumplings (Shao Mai)
recipeInstructions:
Filling A:
5 oz Regular or firm tofu
1 1/2 ts Tientsin preserved cabbage, minced
1 tb Presoaked & minced tree ears
1 tb Presoaded & minced lily buds
3 tb Black or shiitake mushrooms (presoaked & minced)
1 1/2 ts Green onion, minced
1 ts Sesame oil
1 ts Vegetable oil
1/8 ts Salt
2 ts Soy sauce
Filling B:
3 tb Water chestnuts, minced
3 tb Black mushrooms, minced
3 tb Bamboo shoots, minced
3 tb Carrot, minced
2 ts Green onion, minced
1/2 ts Gingerroot, minced
1 tb Soy sauce
1/4 ts Cornstarch
1 1/2 ts Sesame oil
Dipping Sauce:
Soy sauce
Mushroom soaking liquid
Sesame oil
To prepare wrappers, combine flour and hot water. Knead into a smooth dough; cover and let rest at least 1 hour. Place on a lightly floured board, and knead for 2 minutes or so. Roll into a long, cylindrical shape (7-1/2 inches long, 1 inch in diameter). Cut crosswise into 1/2-inch pieces (you will have 15). Shape each, cut-side up, into a round shape. Flatten with the palm of your hand on a flour-dusted board. Roll each into a round wrapper (3-1/2 inches in diameter), thinner toward the edge.
Prepare Filling A or B by combining ingredients. Place approximately 1 tablespoon filling on the center of each wrapper. Hold the wrapper with left fingers, encircle from below with right thumb and index finger, gathering the wrapper around the filling. Squeeze gently to make a neck; some filling should emerge at the top. The bundle should hold together securely or it will collapse during steaming.
Place a layer of damp cloth in a bamboo steaming basket or on a flat, perforated surface (use a heatproof plate if neither is available). Arrange the shao mai on it. Steam for 10 minutes (if frozen, do not defrost first). Serve while hot, before the skin hardens, with dipping sauces made of soy sauce and mushroom soaking liquid (from black mushrooms), mixed in equal proportions and a few drops of sesame oil. These can be assembled in advance, frozen, and steamed just prior to serving.
